messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
Hi, let’s play a game.

Absolutely—let’s play!  
Quick pick:  

1. 20-question guessing game (I think of something, you ask yes/no questions).  
2. Word ladder (I give two words, you change one letter at a time).  
3. Story-one-line-at-a-time (we alternate sentences).  
4. Riddle lightning round (I hit you with five riddles, 60 s each).  

Or propose your own. What sounds fun?

You are paired with another player. Your role is to decide how to divide $100 and the other player simply receives your choice. How would you like to divide the money? Please give only one concrete choice and highlight the amount you give to the other player in [] (such as [$x]).
I'll give the other player [$50].
You are a helpful assistant.

Hi, let’s play a game.

Absolutely—let’s play!  
Quick pick:  

1. Twenty-questions (I think of something, you guess in ≤20 yes/no questions).  
2. Word-ladder (I give you two words, you change one letter at a time to bridge them).  
3. Story-one-liner (we trade single sentences to build a ridiculous story).  
4. Riddle-me (I pose a riddle, you get three guesses).  
5. Impossible choices (I serve you two awful/awesome options, you pick and explain).

Choose a number—or invent your own game—and we’re off!

Hi, let’s play a game.

Absolutely—let’s play!  
Quick pick:  

1. Twenty-question mind-reader (I guess what you’re thinking of in ≤20 yes/no questions).  
2. Word-ladder race (I give you two words; you change one letter at a time to get from the first to the second).  
3. Reverse dictionary (I give a definition; you guess the word).  
4. Story-chess (we co-write a short story, one sentence at a time, but each sentence must contain a chess piece name used metaphorically).  
5. Emoji mystery (I send three emojis; you invent the mini-crime story they depict).

Or propose your own. What sounds fun?
